human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for home-services customer-care boundary discipline, healthcare source quality, PHI and patient-safety handling, referral and authorization prerequisites, clinical and payer interpretation escalation, repeat-failure routing, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to administrative home health and in-home care customer support, service disposition, communication, and escalation packaging grounded in visit, referral, authorization, eligibility, care-transition, privacy, and CRM records.

Authority sources cover federal home health conditions of participation, Medicare home health coverage and benefit policy, HHS OCR HIPAA minimum necessary and TPO disclosure guidance, CMS eligibility and prior authorization posture, OASIS data sets, and CAQH CORE operating rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope resolution, missing facts, policy-exception asks, referral and authorization prerequisite gaps, PHI-sensitive requests, clinical and payer boundary refusal, coding and billing handoff, repeat failure escalation, terminology fidelity, conflicting-source handling, tenant adaptation, and communication handoff.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/home-services-and-field-consumer-services/home-services-customer-care-specialist/ so customer-care work stays separate from clinical review, utilization management, patient access, privacy, coding, revenue cycle, payer operations, field supervision, and approval ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained explicit around PHI disclosure, clinical judgment, medical necessity, payer interpretation, authorization approval, coding, claims, denials, appeals, compensation, and service recovery.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated home-services customer-care specialist for home health and medically adjacent in-home care support resolution.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained-implementation runtime exists.
